data.title: S&P upgrades Quad/Graphics on active debt repayment, improved leverage
data.description:
• S&P Global Ratings upgraded its issuer credit rating for Quad/Graphics (QUAD) to BB- from B+, with a stable outlook, on active debt repayment and improved leverage.
  • The rating agency also raised its issue-level rating on the company's senior secured debt to BB-.
  • The upgrade is driven by Quad's active debt repayment and improved leverage, and strong cost management practices amid ongoing revenue pressures.
  • Considering Quad's rapid debt repayment, S&P now expects the marketing experience company to reduce its reported net leverage to 1.5x (approximately 2.3x on an S&P Global Ratings-adjusted basis) by 2024-end.
  • The stable outlook reflects S&P's expectation that the company's S&P Global Ratings-adjusted leverage will remain below 3x and its free operating cash flow to debt will remain above 15% over the next 12 months.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Patterson jumps 34% on $4.1B Patient Square's take private deal
data.description:
• Patterson Companies (NASDAQ:PDCO) stock jumped 34% in premarket trade, after it said it will be acquired by Patient Square Capital in a $4.1B take private deal.
  • Under the deal, the firm will get $31.35 in cash per share, which is a 35.7% premium to the company's last close on Tuesday.
  • The deal will be funded through a combination of committed equity financing provided by Patient Square Equity Partners, as well as committed debt financing to be led by Citi, UBS Investment Bank, and Wells Fargo Bank N.A.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the fourth quarter of PDCO's fiscal 2025, and after completion it will become a privately held company.
  • Patterson will maintain its headquarters in St. Paul, Minnesota, the company said.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Macy's tops Q3 estimates; lowers FY earnings forecast on accounting error
Nvidia, AMD, Intel invest in Ayar Labs, valuing startup at $1B: report
data.description:
• Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) and Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) have invested in semiconductor startup Ayar Labs, valuing the company at $1B, Bloomberg reported.
  • Ayar Labs, which is based in San Jose, uses light that allows for data to be transferred between processors faster and could boost the trio's artificial intelligence accelerators by making them more efficient.
  • “The AI workload is really breaking the back of the existing hardware infrastructure, especially in interconnects,” Ayar Labs Chief Executive Mark Wade told the news outlet. “We’ve come up with a way to replace those electrical interconnects.”
  • Altogether, Ayar Labs raised $155M in new funding, with Nvidia, AMD Ventures and Intel Capital joining the round. Leading the round were Light Street Capital and Advent Global Opportunities, the news outlet reported.
  • Valued at more than $3T, Nvidia has upped the use of its balance sheet to invest in companies in recent memory. Earlier this month, Nvidia was among a group of investors that invested in the $700M private placement financing round by Nebius (NBIS). The Jensen Huang-led company also invested in Applied Digital (APLD) in September.

data.title: MustGrow Biologics proposes ~$2M private placement
data.description:
• MustGrow Biologics (TSXV:MGRO:CA) (OTCQB:MGROF) has proposed a non-brokered private placement of ~2K units, priced at $1K per unit, to raise gross proceeds of ~$2M.
  • The offering includes an option to increase the size of the private placement by $1M.
  • The agricultural biotechnology company intends to close the private placement on or about January 10, 2025.
  • Each unit comprises of $1K principal amount of unsecured convertible debentures and 666 common share purchase warrants.
  • Each debenture may, at the option of the holder, be converted into common shares at $1.50 per share or paid in cash 60 months following the closing date.
  • Each warrant can be exercisable by the holder thereof to acquire one share at $1.90 per share.
  • The debentures will accrue interest at a rate of 8% per annum. Accrued interest shall be payable semi-annually in cash.
  • The proceeds are intended to be used for inventory production for TerraSante, working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ABVC BioPharma gets $200K in cash from OncoX BioPharma
data.description:
• ABVC BioPharma (NASDAQ:ABVC) said it has received a $200K cash payment from OncoX BioPharma as part of its strategic licensing agreement for certain oncology-related products.
  • The payment is the first installment of $5 million in potential licensing fees from OncoX.
  • After this payment, the total accumulated cash payment ABVC has gotten from its three strategic partners for licensing various ABVC products is $546K.

data.title: Macy's slides after updating full-year guidance, concluding accounting investigation
data.description:
Macy's (NYSE:M) fell in early trading on Wednesday after the department store operator issued financial results and a new outlook to reflect revisions to adjust for misstated delivery expense, and the related accrual and tax effects.

As a result of an independent investigation and forensic analysis, Macy's (M) identified that a single employee with responsibility for small package delivery expense accounting intentionally made erroneous accounting accrual entries to hide approximately $151 million of cumulative delivery expenses from the fourth quarter of 2021 through the third quarter of 2024.

For Q3, Macy's (M) net sales were down 3.1%, with comparable sales down 3.0% on an owned basis and down 2.2% on an owned-plus-licensed-plus-marketplace basis. Macy's (M) management noted that the fragrances, dresses and men’s and women’s active apparel categories were strong during the quarter. Macy's (M) go-forward business comparable sales were down 2.6% on an owned basis and down 1.8% on an owned-plus-licensed-plus-marketplace basis.

Looking ahead, Macy's (M) said quarter-to-date, comparable sales continue to trend ahead of third quarter levels across the portfolio. The company sees full-year sales of $22.3 billion to $22.5 billion (midpoint $22.4 billion). Full-year adjusted EPS of $2.25 to $2.50 (midpoint $2.375) is anticipated vs. $2.73 consensus and a prior view for $2.55 to $2.90.

Shares of Macy's (M) fell 8.49% in premarket trading to $15.30 vs. the 52-week trading range of $14.06 to $22.10.

data.title: Momentus files to sell 15.41M shares of Class A common stock for holders
data.description:
• Momentus (NASDAQ:MNTS) filed a prospectus related to the proposed resale of 15.41 million common shares Class A common stock by the selling stockholders.
